title: Menstrual migraine documentation

red_flags.text: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.
red_flags.rationale: Supports entered headache history, change, warning features and diary context.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

source.title: Headaches in over 12s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — assessment, red flags, and headache diary

medication.text: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.
medication.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed migraine features and temporal pattern without assigning a diagnosis.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

source.title: Headaches in over 12s: diagnosis and management

medication.text: Supports documentation that an acute or preventive migraine plan was clinician-decided and that prophylaxis was reviewed; no medicine name or dose is mapped.
medication.rationale: Supports documentation of clinician-selected treatment response and review.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documentation that an acute or preventive migraine plan was clinician-decided and that prophylaxis was reviewed; no medicine name or dose is mapped.

source_limitations: Cycle timing, headache phenotype, neurological findings, pregnancy, medicines and warning features are absent.
No migraine diagnosis, acute treatment, prophylaxis or urgency is generated.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
